title: More than 45 killed, around 70 injured in blast at explosives storage building in Myanmar
content: An explosion at a building reportedly storing mining explosives in northeastern Myanmar killed over 45 people and injured around 70. The blast occurred Sunday in Kaungtup village, near the Chinese border, an area controlled by the TNLA. Rescue efforts are ongoing, with 46 bodies recovered and 74 injured taken to the hospital.

title: TMC defers Mamata-led MLAs' meeting after majority skip amid Abhishek, Kalyan Banerjee attack row
content: A Trinamool Congress (TMC) meeting was postponed after nearly three-fourths of the party's 80 MLAs failed to attend, following alleged attacks on Abhishek Banerjee and Kalyan Banerjee. The party announced statewide rallies and a sit-in in Kolkata against post-poll violence and evictions, blaming BJP for "terror" against workers. BJP denies involvement, citing local anger.

title: Over two dozen BEd candidates injured as drain cover collapses outside Kanpur exam centre
content: Around two dozen Uttar Pradesh BEd Joint Entrance Examination candidates fell into a sewage drain when a damaged concrete slab collapsed outside an examination centre in Kanpur. The incident occurred due to overcrowding near a photocopy shop, causing minor injuries and damage to belongings. Despite the disruption, the examination proceeded as scheduled.

title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi finishes as tournament's highest run-scorer
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, a 15-year-old, has won the IPL 2026 Orange Cap. He scored 776 runs at an astonishing average and strike rate. Sooryavanshi broke records for most sixes and fastest to 1,000 runs. His dominance in the powerplay was also remarkable. This season redefined teenage potential in the IPL.

title: AI's hallucination problem is getting bigger, and what makes it worse
content: AI tools are becoming more convincing, but experts warn their tendency to confidently present false information is a growing concern.  These systems, optimized for plausibility rather than truth, can mislead users in critical areas like research and healthcare.  The risk of errors spreading due to unverified AI responses is significant, making detection harder.

title: Bullion movement: Gold prices to track US–Iran peace talks, Fed signals and key data releases this week
content: Gold prices face a mixed outlook this week, influenced by US-Iran peace talks, oil market shifts, and crucial US economic data. Federal Reserve officials' speeches will also guide interest rate expectations, impacting bullion's near-term trajectory. Analysts suggest a sideways to corrective momentum for precious metals, with progress in peace discussions potentially easing safe-haven demand.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe at Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: ‘Players require rest’: Gavaskar questions IPL final schedule, says it was unfair to GT
content: Sunil Gavaskar has criticized the IPL 2026 final's scheduling, suggesting a postponement due to Gujarat Titans' travel woes.  Disruptions delayed their arrival in Ahmedabad, leaving them with less than 24 hours to prepare after an exhausting playoff run. Gavaskar believes a reserve day should have been utilized for a fairer contest.

title: Hundreds of faculty members of University of California sign open letter
content: University of California faculty are urging the reinstatement of SAT/ACT math requirements for STEM applicants, citing a significant decline in student math skills since the tests were dropped. They argue current admissions practices fail to ensure readiness, forcing instructors to reteach basic math and jeopardizing the quality of STEM education.

title: IPL Final toss verdict: Does choosing to bowl first really work? What history says
content: RCB captain Rajat Patidar opted to bowl first in the IPL 2026 final against Gujarat Titans, sparking debate on the toss advantage. Historically, batting first holds a slight edge in finals, with 10 wins to 8 for chasing teams. However, Patidar trusts his bowlers on a good batting surface, aiming for a win irrespective of past trends.

title: June 2026 bank holidays: When and where will banks remain closed? Check state-wise list
content: Planning a bank visit in June requires checking the holiday list as branches in several states will be closed on specific dates for festivals like YMA Day, Raja Sankranti, Moharrum, and Muharram. Additionally, banks observe closures on the second and fourth Saturdays of each month, impacting physical branch operations.

title: Why the IPL 2026 Final toss left both RCB and GT smiling in Ahmedabad
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru opted to bowl first in the IPL 2026 final against Gujarat Titans. Skipper Rajat Patidar backed his team's strong chasing record on a batting-friendly pitch. Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill also admitted he would have batted first, highlighting the toss's favorable outcome for both sides. One change sees Arshad Khan replace Sai Kishore for GT.

title: India’s domestic air traffic falls 4.2% in April amid weak demand and rising costs
content: Domestic air travel experienced a dip in April, with passenger numbers falling 4.2% from March and 3.47% year-on-year. Airlines faced headwinds from softer demand and rising costs, leading to network adjustments. IndiGo expanded its market share to 65%, while Air India Group saw a decline.  Operational performance varied, with IndiGo leading on-time flights.

title: PSG vs Arsenal is proof that the Gunners can slow down time
content: PSG won the trophy, Arsenal won the low block, and neutrals lost 120 minutes they may never emotionally recover from.

title: CBSE vs students: Board admits flaws after teen hacker exposes website vulnerabilities
content: CBSE has addressed security vulnerabilities found in its OnMark portal, thanking ethical hackers for their assistance. A cybersecurity team, including experts from government agencies and IITs, has been deployed to strengthen the system. The statement comes after 19-year-old ethical hacker Nisarga Adhikary alleged security flaws in CBSE's digital evaluation ecosystem.

title: 'Ignore positive news and youngsters may end up following the cockroach': VP Radhakrishnan
content: Vice president C P Radhakrishnan urged media to highlight positive achievements, warning that neglecting them could lead youth to follow fleeting trends like the "cockroach." He stressed the importance of constructive reporting to inspire societal progress and nation-building, emphasizing that genuinely good stories resonate long-term, unlike ephemeral social media fads.

title: Ball that goes both ways: Tendulkar explains Bhuvi's 'wobble' magic - Watch
content: Bhuvneshwar has played a key role in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's run to the IPL 2026 final, taking 26 wickets in 15 matches. Ahead of Sunday's title clash against Gujarat Titans at Narendra Modi Stadium, Tendulkar explained how the veteran seamer has adapted his approach this season.

title: Watch: Massive dust wall engulfs Jaisalmer, darkens skies
content: A colossal dust whirlwind, originating near the India-Pakistan border, engulfed Jaisalmer's canal and border regions on Sunday.  Ramgarh, Mohangarh, and PTM town experienced near-darkness as the storm reduced visibility drastically, forcing daytime headlight use.  This powerful sandstorm, accompanied by high winds, disrupted daily life and caused power outages, offering a stark contrast to the preceding heat.

title: Five Ebola patients recover in Congo as WHO opens new treatment centre
content: Five patients infected with a rare Ebola strain have recovered in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with four set for discharge from a new treatment center. This marks the first documented recovery from the Bundibugyo strain, as the WHO urges early medical attention amidst community resistance and a rapidly spreading outbreak.

title: ‘If he bowls three overs, RCB are gone’: Ex-India captain's explosive IPL Final warning
content: Former India captain Kris Srikkanth has urged Royal Challengers Bengaluru to drop Romario Shepherd for the IPL 2026 final, deeming his bowling a potential liability. He also advised RCB to retain Venkatesh Iyer. Srikkanth believes Gujarat Titans must dismiss Virat Kohli, Devdutt Padikkal, and Rajat Patidar early to stand a chance.

title: Two-year title drought ends! Satwik-Chirag win maiden Singapore Open
content: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty clinched their maiden Singapore Open title, ending a two-year wait for a trophy. The Indian duo staged a remarkable comeback, defeating Indonesia's Fajar Alfian and Muhammad Shohibul Fikri 18-21, 21-17, 21-16 in a thrilling final. This historic victory marks their first doubles title at the prestigious Singapore Open and their ninth World Tour crown.

title: UP gets permanent DGP after 4 years, Rajeev Krishna appointed state police chief
content: Rajeev Krishna, a 1991 batch IPS officer, has been appointed as the new Director General of Police (DGP) of Uttar Pradesh, ending years of uncertainty. Krishna, who previously served as acting DGP, will lead the state's police force with nearly three years of service remaining. His appointment signals a focus on technology-led policing reforms and strengthening operational effectiveness.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is in Ahmedabad despite RR missing the IPL Final
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, a 15-year-old from Rajasthan Royals, is set to be a major attraction at the IPL 2026 final. He has had an exceptional season, leading in runs and sixes. Sooryavanshi is expected to win multiple awards. His performance has redefined T20 batting possibilities. The final will feature Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans.

title: Stock market recap: Seven of top-10 firms lose Rs 1.54 lakh crore
content: Seven of India's top 10 companies experienced a combined market valuation drop of Rs 1.54 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries leading the decline. This occurred as the BSE Sensex and NSE Nifty saw minor dips. Despite the losses, Reliance Industries maintained its position as the country's most-valued firm.

title: IPL Final: Home advantage or batting depth — where the title may ultimately be decided
content: In a nail-biting conclusion to IPL 2026, the Royal Challengers Bengaluru face off against the formidable Gujarat Titans, two teams that have dominated the season with their impressive performances. With top-order hitters ready to set the stage ablaze and fast bowlers eager to claim wickets, the final at Ahmedabad promises a spectacle.

title: Middle East conflict is rewriting your kitchen math — Explained in 10 charts
content: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting household budgets, driving up prices for everyday essentials like cooking oil, milk, and bread. Soaring global crude oil prices and a weakening rupee are increasing import, fuel, and transportation costs, leading to a domino effect on consumer goods and even healthcare services.

title: Rajat Patidar: The injury replacement turned captain who changed the fate of RCB
content: Soft-spoken away from the spotlight and measured in his demeanour, Rajat Patidar never fit the image of a franchise-defining superstar. Yet the injury replacement who once went unsold at auction became the captain who delivered RCB's maiden IPL title. Now, after another evolution in his leadership and batting, Patidar stands one victory away from achieving what only Rohit Sharma and MS Dhoni have managed.

title: At least 5 killed after collapse at illegal mining operation in China
content: A collapse at an illegal mining operation in China's Yunnan province on Sunday resulted in at least five fatalities and one injury. Six individuals were trapped, with all recovered and taken to the hospital. Authorities are investigating the cause of the incident, which follows a deadly coal mine explosion in Shanxi province.

title: 'A chat with Soros agents': Rahul's dig at BJP as he meets CBSE students
content: Rahul Gandhi met CBSE Class 12 students labelled 'anti-national' after questioning an incorrect Physics answer sheet. The board later admitted an error. Gandhi criticised the government's response, stating the students deserve a secure future and that problems can't be solved by denying them. The incident highlighted scrutiny of the board's evaluation process.

title: 'Began firing indiscriminately': How UP police gunned down key accused in Ghaziabad teen murder
content: A 17-year-old murder suspect, Asad, carrying a Rs 50,000 reward, was killed in a police encounter in Ghaziabad's Khoda Colony. Asad was wanted for the stabbing death of a teenager earlier this week. During a chase, Asad and an associate opened fire on police, leading to a shootout where Asad was fatally injured. A constable was also wounded.

title: India-US trade talks to resume on June 1 as both sides push to finalise interim pact
content: Senior officials from India and the US are meeting in New Delhi to finalize an interim trade agreement and advance broader bilateral trade talks. Discussions will cover market access, customs, and investment, with potential recalibration of the deal due to recent US tariff changes. The agreement aims to boost trade and economic security between the two nations.

title: Tendulkar's major IPL suggestion: Scrap Impact Player rule, allow 5 overs per bowler
content: Cricket legend Sachin Tendulkar has advocated for significant IPL rule changes, including the removal of the Impact Player rule, which he believes heavily favors batters. Tendulkar also proposed a bowling powerplay and allowing one bowler to deliver five overs to restore game balance.

title: Who replaces DKS as Karnataka Congress chief? Kharge says search is on
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge stated that the party is actively searching for a new Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) president to strengthen the organization for upcoming elections. Discussions regarding the new Karnataka cabinet composition are ongoing, with a phased expansion potentially occurring after the June 3 swearing-in ceremony.

title: Dalal Street outlook: What will guide market sentiments this week?
content: Dalal Street anticipates a busy week driven by the RBI's interest rate decision, key economic data, and global developments like the US-Iran situation and crude oil prices. Investors will closely track manufacturing and services PMI, GDP growth, and foreign exchange reserves for market direction.

title: With assets worth Rs 1,413 crore, DK Shivakumar set to become India's richest CM
content: Karnataka's D K Shivakumar has become the wealthiest chief minister in India, with declared assets totaling Rs 1,413 crore. His wealth has significantly increased since 2008, largely due to inherited land and property appreciation. Shivakumar's assets include substantial immovable and movable properties, with liabilities of Rs 265 crore.

title: As ACs run nonstop in 45°C heat, are Delhi homes facing a growing fire hazard?
content: Delhi-NCR's sweltering heat is pushing ACs to their limits, sparking a rise in dangerous fires. Faulty wiring, overloaded circuits, and poor maintenance are increasingly causing AC-related blazes, with recent fatal incidents highlighting the urgent need for awareness. Experts warn that ignoring early warning signs like burning smells or flickering lights can have devastating consequences.

title: FPI profile: Foreign investors continue selling spree in May, pull out Rs 32,000 crore
content: Foreign portfolio investors continued their exit from Indian equities in May, withdrawing Rs 32,963 crore due to concerns over earnings growth and a weakening rupee. This outflow has pushed cumulative FPI withdrawals in 2026 to Rs 2.25 lakh crore, surpassing the entire previous year's figure. While selling intensity has eased, a turnaround in flows is unlikely without significant macroeconomic improvement.

title: ‘Nexbax AI Index’ to redefine AI adoption in India
content: It argues that the metrics dominating AI development in the world are essentially useless for the billion-plus users across India and the Global South

title: Unified Shakti smart card planned to enable seamless travel on BMTC buses and Namma Metro for women
content: Officials said that the new card is being designed on the National Common Mobility Card (NCMC) platform, a nationwide standard that allows commuters to access various public transport systems through a single smart card.

title: Students in Kerala to take cyber safety pledge on school reopening day
content: The initiative is being implemented as part of the ‘Kid Glove project’, a programme of the Kerala Police Cyber Wing to create awareness about the pitfalls of the cyber world among students

title: CBSE’s three-language formula triggers concerns among students, parents
content: Stakeholders fear mandatory inclusion of two Indian languages from Class IX could disrupt academic plans, limit foreign language options and increase pressure on learners

title: Corporation faces flak for waterlogging in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Mayor maintains that the flooding was not caused due to the drains under the Corporation’s control; Water Resources Minister to chair meeting on the issue

title: West Bengal Cabinet expansion: 35 more Ministers to be sworn in on June 1
content: With this expansion on June 1, Cabinet strength will rise to 41; along with seniority, the BJP is likely to give considerations to regional and community-wise representations

title: Varying weather pattern may continue in Andhra Pradesh
content: While light showers, along with thunderstorms, are expected in Rayalaseema, hot weather may persist in other parts of the State; Atchampeta in Palnadu district records the day’s highest maximum temperature of 44.3°C

title: Rahul targets Centre over CBSE OSM row, alleges ‘fraud’ in scanning process
content: In a separate social media post, Congress communication chief Jairam Ramesh alleged a major data breach in the CBSE’s Class 12 examination system, claiming that answer sheets of nearly two million students were available in the public domain.

title: 15-day ultimatum to Rabri Devi to vacate Patna bungalow
content: RJD leaders say Minister Ram should question why government is repeatedly changing his residence, after he claimed Rabri Devi was refusing to vacate because he was from the Dalit community

title: Discussions under way to appoint new KPCC chief, says Kharge
content: The Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha addressed key queries regarding the formation of a new council of Ministers and the appointment of a new State unit president, and rumours surrounding multiple Deputy Chief Minister posts

title: Odisha government clears key road for Vedanta’s mining project
content: State government approves road construction in favour of State-run Odisha Industrial Infrastructure Development Corporation (IDCO) after Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change granted Stag-II or final approval in favour of road project.

title: Huge fire destroys two units in an automobile factory in Sriperumbudur
content: At about 3.30 p.m., a sudden fire erupted in the Hyundai Mobis factory’s waste storage area; fire and rescue services personnel brought under control after more than four hours of operation in which two units in the factory have been completely destroyed.

title: Despite social media build-up, Cockroach Janata Party meeting in Kochi fails to take place
content: Kochi police say they received information that the meeting proposed on Marine Drive on Sunday afternoon was called off

title: Pawan Kalyan announces formation of Sena Galam
content: It is aimed at fostering social harmony and responsible politics, and voicing the aspirations of Gen Z, he says

title: Attack on ED officials: SFI Thiruvananthapuram district president arrested
content: SFI leadership deny Vijay Vimal’s involvement in the incident; police, however, maintain that video footage from the area conclusively proves his role in the assault

title: SIR enumeration phase begins in Odisha, Mizoram, Sikkim and Manipur
content: Enumeration forms must be submitted by June 28 to be included in draft electoral rolls; this is part of the third phase of the SIR, which will complete the process across the country except Himachal, J&K, Ladakh

title: Inter-State foetal sex determination racket busted in Chittoor; four arrested
content: The racket was discovered during a surprise raid by the Police and Health Department officials at an under-construction house near Chandrasekharapuram village near Bangarupalem, says SP

title: Tight security for Vijay’s visit to Tiruchi today
content: 2,500 police personnel have been deployed for the security arrangement; Anand appeals to Vijay supporters without QR passes not to come to the meeting venue

title: Kottayam, Pathanamthitta intensify monsoon preparedness efforts
content: Kollam Collector Chetan Kumar Meena directed the Local Self-Government department and other agencies to take urgent steps to remove hazardous trees and branches along roadsides and in public spaces

title: EAGLE Head urges youth to avoid tobacco at awareness rally
content: Police, NGOs and officials of various government departments take out rally from NTR Collectorate to Indira Gandhi Municipal Corporation Stadium to mark ‘World No Tobacco Day’

title: Nedumangad child murder case: crowd attacks accused during evidence collection
content: Autopsy of the one-and-a-half-year-old child reveals 51 injuries across the body, including fresh wounds and partially healed scars; doctors confirm that physical trauma was the definitive cause of death

title: Strict action in Nedumangad child murder case: Minister
content: Bindhu Krishna promises that the perpetrators will face certain and severe punishment, reiterates that her department is working on preventative measures to ensure such horrific acts against children never repeat

title: Community drawing campaign to be held in Kozhikode on June 5
content: Kozhikode UNESCO City of Literature is partnering with ‘The Colors of Childhood Inspiration 2026’, an initiative organised by the Ulyanovsk City of Literature in Russia

title: Thiruvilwamala palm leaf records throw new light on Kerala history
content: The palm leaf documents, preserved as Granthavaris, had remained largely unread in recent memory and were traditionally brought out only for occasional ritual purposes

title: End of an era for Air India colonies in Mumbai’s Kalina as last of the occupants leave
content: the first staff colony was followed by three more through the 198th century . the colony is the first of the four constructed way back in 1955 . on Sunday, the last of the occupants will leave, and Mumbai International Airport Ltd will take possession of the land leased to it two decades ago .

title: The big Hapus crisis: How climate shocks are threatening India's iconic Alphonso mango
content: alphonso mangoes are grown across parts of the Konkan belt and beyond . farmers are struggling to meet demand, and the second is protecting the identity of the Ratnagiri Hapus . crates of the golden fruit were a reminder that summer had arrived .

title: Google's 32 Million-Strong Mosquito Army To Fight...Mosquitoes
content: The technique of sterilising pest insects to control their numbers is not a new one and has been discussed as a chemical-free alternative for decades.

title: Vice President's 'Youngsters Will Follow The Cockroach' Warning To Media
content: C P Radhakrishnan said positive developments should receive greater attention in the media so that young people are exposed to the right information and role models.

title: Ladakh's New Excise Policy Raises Liquor Outlets From 2 To 20, Eases Rules
content: According to the Ladakh administration, it's for the first time that retail vending of liquor has been allowed in guest houses and homestays on payment of licence fees

title: Attack On Abhishek Banerjee In Bengal Gives INDIA Bloc Fresh Momentum
content: Abhishek Banerjee was allegedly set upon by locals and beaten up when he tried to visit the family of a party leader who allegedly died in poll violence.

title: Video: Huge Explosion Rocks Myanmar Building, Children Among 45 Dead
content: About 70 other people were injured in the explosion that took place around noon in the village of Kaungtup, in Namhkam township.

title: Food Regulator Seeks Explanation From Blinkit On Bad Quality Curd Complaint
content: The FSSAI has directed Blinkit to provide an explanation and also submit a comprehensive Action Taken Report (ATR).

title: Medieval Castle Captured In Israel's Deepest Push Into Lebanon In Decades
content: The capture of Beaufort castle came after days of airstrikes and intense fighting in nearby villages where Israeli troops fought Hezbollah members.

title: 39 Hardinge Road: The Reason Why Rabri Devi Doesn't Want To Vacate Patna Bungalow
content: Rabri Devi, 67, doesn't want her political career to go south; hence, she is trying to avoid the so-called unlucky house altogether.

title: Why Activist Manoj Jarange Keeps Maharashtra Governments On Edge
content: The continuing relevance of Manoj Jarange stems from the fact that he has become the face of a demand that sits at the intersection of identity, social justice, constitutional law and electoral politics

title: The Night Paris Plunged Into Chaos: Riots, Death, Mayhem Grip City Streets
content: Paris Saint-Germain retained the Champions League title on Saturday, beating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in Budapest, but the joy of victory gave way to destruction on the streets of France's capital.

title: Gautam Adani Bets On AI-Driven Infrastructure Boom In Letter To Shareholders
content: In his annual letter to shareholders, Gautam Adani said the conglomerate remained committed to expansion despite heightened scrutiny over the past year.

title: Man Injured In Ambush By Kuki Insurgents, Alleges Naga Village Body In Manipur
content: Manipur ambush: The Konsakhul Village Authority asked the government to take "immediate and decisive measures" to ensure the safety of residents, restore essential services

title: 'Destroy More, Get More': Ukraine Turns Drone Warfare Into Video Game
content: Through its Army of Drones Bonus program, frontline units earn points for verified battlefield achievements

title: Video: Moment South Delhi Building Collapsed Like A House Of Cards
content: Rescuers from government agencies and local volunteers worked through the night as they looked for survivors and cleared the debris

title: Video: Russian Forces Use Handheld "Yolka" System To Take Down Drone
content: The system first came into public attention during Russia’s Victory Day parade in May 2025, when a compact device was seen with a security officer near President Vladimir Putin.

title: 'Uphold Iran's Rights': Top Iranian Negotiator's Condition To Sign US Deal
content: Iran views sanction relief and the release of its assets frozen in banks abroad as among its key rights to be ensured under any deal with the US.

title: AI Solves 80-Year-Old Math Problem That Stumped Experts For Decades
content: OpenAI's AI model solved the famous unit distance problem, a question that had challenged mathematicians since 1946

title: Narrower Than A Fridge, Deadlier Than Thai Caves: Inside Laos Cave Rescue
content: The questions outnumbered solutions. Were the miners safe, or even alive? Could they be reached? How would they use regulators, gauges and oxygen tanks, diving equipment they probably would have never seen, let alone used?

title: Top India, US Negotiators To Hold 4-Day Trade Talks In Delhi From Tomorrow
content: The US team will be led by its chief negotiator Brendan Lynch. India's chief negotiator is Darpan Jain, who is an additional secretary in the Department of Commerce.

title: Woman Turns Down Proposal, Man Kills Her 1-Year-Old Son, Then An Encounter
content: The accused admitted that he wanted to marry the child's mother, but she had refused, saying she already had a child.
